competition: Scaling performance of quantum computing hardware: CR&D

official_ifs_requirements.requirement: Collaborative project lead must be a UK registered business or research and technology organisation.
official_ifs_requirements.source: Official IFS Quantum Hardware page lines 110-111

official_ifs_requirements.requirement: Collaborators are invited into IFS and must form an eligible collaboration with at least one organisation claiming funding; one partner cannot account for more than 75 percent of eligible costs.
official_ifs_requirements.source: Official IFS Quantum Hardware page lines 121-132

official_ifs_requirements.requirement: Scope must be directly relevant to exploitation of second generation quantum technologies for universal fault tolerant quantum computing.
official_ifs_requirements.source: Official IFS Quantum Hardware page lines 240-242

official_ifs_requirements.requirement: Funded themes include below-threshold error rate, scalable control/input-output, syndrome processing latency/throughput, and modular interconnect/qubit networking hardware.
official_ifs_requirements.source: Official IFS Quantum Hardware page lines 248-255

official_ifs_requirements.requirement: Projects that do not provide a hardware solution for fault-tolerant quantum computers or do not address specific capability gaps will not be funded.
official_ifs_requirements.source: Official IFS Quantum Hardware page lines 258-263

local_evidence_readback.finding: Aureon partner pack exists for assurance, reproducibility, benchmark traceability and reporting; it explicitly requires a quantum hardware lead and hardware access.
Portal-ready text is staged language only and does not evidence hardware ownership, hardware access, consortium budget or eligible partner invites.
Local abstract states the route is not a standalone Aureon submission and requires a quantum hardware lead partner, device-level work packages, hardware milestones and scale evidence.
Earlier unlock audit found only planning/index files, not a quantum hardware lead partner commitment.

drive_searches.result_summary: Returned Aureon War Room/planning files, master index, research documents and grant package files. No hardware lead partner commitment or hardware access evidence found.
No route-specific evidence surfaced from Drive metadata searches.
No confirmed hardware partner or access evidence surfaced from Drive metadata searches.
No confirmed hardware partner or access evidence surfaced from Drive metadata searches.

route_action.reason: Official scope is quantum hardware CR&D for fault-tolerant quantum computing. Current Aureon evidence supports a possible assurance work package only and does not evidence hardware lead, hardware access, device work packages, consortium budget or partner invites.

next_actions: Do not submit the Quantum Hardware route as a standalone R&A/Aureon application.
Use the staged partner abstract only to obtain a named quantum hardware business or RTO lead, device/access evidence, capability gap, hardware-solution scope and eligible-cost budget.
Only move to IFS drafting after partner invite details and cost shares are evidenced.
controls: No unsupported quantum hardware lead or access claim made
No unsupported device, fault-tolerant hardware, benchmark, partner invite or budget claim made
No IFS portal mutation, external email, upload, partner invite or final submission performed
